mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals accelerating consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are non-positive in >50% of walk-forward observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers recessionary discounting by equity investors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of occurrences during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal a stagflationary environment where monetary tightening coincides with rising input costs, compressing profit margins and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases MoM and CPIAUCSL increases MoM
failure_condition: When both proxies increase simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or rank correlation > 0 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when industrial production (INDPRO) is above its 12-month trailing average, positive momentum in industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity that supports broad earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds its 12-month average, positive MoM changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 5-day forward returns in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following any rate increase.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in new private housing starts (HOUST) reflects durable construction demand and positive household formation, leading to cyclical equity outperformance and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive MoM changes in HOUST coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of monthly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 0.5 percent signals an accelerating macroeconomic expansion that reduces recession risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 0.5%, subsequent 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm broad-based demand strength across consumer and investment sectors, amplifying cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases MoM and HOUST increases MoM
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase simultaneously, 5-day forward returns are non-positive in >50% of triggered observations during walk-forward testing.
